commentaries.author: Augustine of Hippo
commentaries.content: "Blessed is the man whom Thou chastenest, O Lord: and teachest him from Thy law" (ver. Behold, thou hast the counsel of God, wherefore He spareth the wicked: the pit is being digged for the sinner. Thou wishest to bury him at once: the pit is as yet being dug for him: do not be in haste to bury him. What mean the words, "until the pit be digged up for the sinner ". or whom cloth He mean by sinner. The whole race of such that are sinners. No; them that are proud; for he had said before, "Reward the proud after their deserving. " For that publican, who would not so much as lift up his eyes to heaven, but "smote upon his breast, saying, God be merciful to me a sinner," was a sinner; but since he was not proud, and since God will render a recompense to the proud; the pit is being dug not for him, but for them that are such, until He render a recompense to the proud. In the words then, "until the pit be digged up for the ungodly," understand the proud. Who is the proud. He who doth not by confession of his sins do penance, that he may be healed through his humility. He who chooseth to arrogate to himself those few good things which he seemeth to possess, and who doth detract from the mercy of God. He who although he doth ascribe unto God his good works, yet insulteth those who do not those good works, and raiseth himself above them. This then is the Christian doctrine: no man doeth anything well except by His grace. A man's bad acts are his own: his good he doth of God's bounty. When he hath begun to do well, let not him ascribe it unto himself: when he hath not attributed it to himself, let him give thanks to Him from whom he hath received it. But when he doeth well, let him not insult him who doth not as he doth nor exalt himself above him: for the grace of, God is not stayed at him, so that it cannot reach another.
